name: Brandenburg (Germany)
description: Two equal horizontal stripes of red over white, with the red eagle of Brandenburg charged on the centre.
history: Adopted on 30 January 1991 by the re-established Land of Brandenburg following German reunification, restoring the historic red-and-white colours and the medieval red eagle. The state had been dissolved by the East German government in 1952 and reconstituted in 1990.

features.symbolism: Red and white are the historic colours of the March of Brandenburg, taken from the red eagle on white that has been the territorial emblem since the medieval Margraviate.

features.role: Brandenburg red eagle (Roter Adler), displayed with golden beak, tongue, and claws
features.symbolism: The red eagle has been the heraldic emblem of Brandenburg since the 12th century, used by the Ascanian margraves and continuously since.
